Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Conduct Mental Examination Of Plaintiff
Off Calendar Apr-19-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Cody Christiansen (510-646-1342) of Harris Personal Injury Lawyers, Inc. for Plaintiff.
Aron Liang for Defendant.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT RICHARD FIDLER's Motion For Leave To Conduct Mental Examination Of Plaintiff:

Defendant's motion is granted subject to the following limitations: since plaintiff's deposition has been taken, plaintiff may not be questioned during the examination regarding the history of the accident (description of events underlying the conditions), or other history to the extent testimony on these topics has been provided by plaintiff in deposition. Defendant is ordered to provide clarification and/or narrow the list of tests that may be administered during the examination. The deposition is to be done in one day, parties are to continue to meet and confer, parties are to respect Covid protocols and keep in mind the availability of the doctor's time.

Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to jskrmpotic@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Stephanie Krmpotic, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
